onboarding.title: Train solo. Level up for real.
onboarding.step0: Pick a program, complete today’s habits, log workouts, then finish your weekly challenge. Tap Next when you’re ready.
onboarding.step1: Works fully offline on your phone after install. Everything saves on this device only — nothing is uploaded. Back up anytime from the You tab.
onboarding.step2: Pick a display name. You can switch language anytime under the You tab.

command.fatigueHint: If fatigue is high, ease up until it drops. Tap Rest below.
command.programHint: {{name}} · Week {{week}} — change program from Programs tab
command.weeklyGoalUnset: Set a goal under the You tab (sessions or manual progress).

dungeon.title: Choose your focus
dungeon.intro: Each card is a training style. Pick one, then tap Next week after a full week of sessions.
dungeon.nextWeekHint: You are on week {{week}}. Tap after your planned week is complete.

gates.title: Small wins (daily)
gates.intro: Check items when done. They reset every calendar day. Earn XP once per item.
gates.accessory: Complete one extra lift you usually skip

log.title: Save your session
log.intro: Logging adds XP and raises fatigue (like hard training). Use Rest on Home when you feel drained.
log.introDetail: Add exercises and sets (weight × reps). Tap the box for a photo if you don’t know the name (browser or app). Optional RPE, rest timer, repeat last session. On iPhone Safari you can Add to Home Screen — see You tab.
log.empty: No workouts yet — log your first session.
log.exerciseNamePh: Name (optional if you add a photo)
log.validationBody: Add at least one exercise with a name or photo and weight/reps, or write notes for a quick entry.
log.repeatNoneBody: Save a workout first, then you can clone it.
log.photoMessage: Choose a picture so you recognize the move later.
log.photoDeniedBody: Allow photo library or camera access (app Settings or browser permissions) to attach pictures.
log.flowHint: 1) Name this workout (e.g. Leg day). 2) Tap My exercises or + Exercise to add moves. 3) Optional photo. 4) Enter weight and reps for each set.
log.myExercisesHint: Tap a card to add it here. Use Save to my exercises under a move to keep it in this list.
log.myExercisesEmpty: Nothing saved yet — add name or photo below, then tap Save to my exercises.
log.removeMyExerciseBody: Only removes this shortcut. Past workouts stay the same.
log.savedOk: Workout saved — scroll down to see it in Recent workouts.
log.hubIntro: Create a page for each focus (for example Leg day). Open it to log sessions and browse only that page’s history — repeat-last stays on the same page.
log.hubEmpty: No pages yet — add one above to start logging.
log.deletePlanBody: Removes “{{name}}” from your list. Past sessions stay saved; they won’t show under this page if you delete it.
log.planPageIntro: Everything here is saved under this page only. Repeat last clones your last session on this page.
log.planHistoryEmpty: No sessions on this page yet — save your first workout below.
log.savedOkPlan: Saved — scroll down for this page’s history.
log.repeatNonePlanBody: Log at least one workout on this page first, then you can repeat it.
log.equipTagsHint: Optional tags for where you train (saved with this page).
log.prThisMonth: Best this month on this page: {{weight}} × {{reps}}

profile.title: Rank {{rank}}
profile.intro: Numbers below are simple motivators (not medical advice).
profile.resetHint: Deletes saved progress on this phone and returns to the welcome screens.
profile.weeklyGoalHint: Resets each calendar week. Sessions mode counts saved workouts. Manual mode uses + / −.
profile.backupHint: Export downloads or shares a JSON file. Import replaces all data in this browser or app.
profile.backupEmailHint: On your phone, tap Export then choose Mail or Messages to send the file to yourself.
profile.backupShareUnavailable: Sharing is not available on this device.
profile.homeScreenBody: Add Solo Rep to your home screen (Share → Add to Home Screen on iPhone, or your launcher’s shortcut option on Android) for faster logging at the gym.
profile.pwaBody: Open this site in Safari, tap Share, then Add to Home Screen. It opens full screen like an app. You need an internet connection unless your host enables offline caching.
profile.bossDefaultDetail: Add a little weight or one rep on your main lift, or repeat last week with better form.

volume.name: Muscle & volume
volume.focus: Muscle growth · steady overload · volume wins

spire.name: Pure strength
spire.focus: Big lifts · controlled intensity · technique first

ruins.name: Comeback / rehab
ruins.focus: Pain‑free range · slow progress · rest days matter

feed.bossCleared: [System] Weekly challenge complete — big XP bonus.

scenario.full:
You’re building a habit loop, not chasing motivation.

Solo Rep is a training tracker with light RPG flavor: sessions, daily habits, recovery, steady progression, and one bigger test each week.

• Pick a program that matches your goal.
• Use Today for small daily checks between hard workouts.
• Workouts is your log — honest notes beat fantasy stats.
• Fatigue reminds you to recover.
• Weekly challenge is your focused push (progression, AMRAP, or conditioning).

Data stays on your device; use Backup on the You tab to keep a copy safe.
